article_id: 1. There shall be a President of Nauru, who shall be elected by Parliament.
text_snippet: 2. A person is not qualified to be elected President unless he is a member of Parliament.

article_id: 1. This Constitution is the supreme law of Nauru.
text_snippet: 2. A law inconsistent with this Constitution is, to the extent of the inconsistency, void. PART II. PROTECTION OF FUNDAMENTAL RIGHTS AND FREEDOMS

article_id: 17. Executive Authority Vested in the Cabinet.
text_snippet: 1. The executive authority of Nauru is vested in a Cabinet constituted as provided by this Part and the Cabinet has the general direction and control of the government of Nauru.

article_id: 21. Provision for Minister to act as President.
text_snippet: The Cabinet may appoint a Minister to perform the duties and exercise the functions of the President during any period during which the President is unable to act owing to illness, absence from Nauru
The President may assign to himself or to a Minister responsibility for any business of the government of Nauru and may revoke or vary an assignment made under this Article.

There shall be a Parliament of Nauru.
Subject to this Constitution, Parliament may make laws for the peace, order and good government of Nauru; laws so made may have effect outside as well as within Nauru.

1. Parliament shall consist of eighteen members or such greater number as is prescribed by law. 2. For the purpose of the election of members of Parliament, Nauru shall be divided into constituencies.
Members of Parliament shall be elected in such manner as is prescribed by law, by Nauruan citizens who have attained the age of twenty years.

article_id: 36. Determinations and Questions of Membership of Parliament.
text_snippet: Any question that arises concerning the right of a person to be or to remain a member of Parliament shall be referred to and determined by the Supreme Court.

article_id: 37. Powers, Privileges and Immunities of Parliament.
text_snippet: The powers, privileges and immunities of Parliament and of its members and committees are such as are declared by Parliament.
